sanskrit_devanagari:
शुचौ देशे प्रतिष्ठाप्य स्थिरमासनमात्मनः ।
नात्युच्छ्रितं नातिनीचं चैलाजिनकुशोत्तरम् ॥ ११ ॥
sanskrit_iast:
śucau deśe pratiṣṭhāpya sthiram āsanam ātmanaḥ |
nāty-ucchritaṁ nāti-nīcaṁ cailājina-kuśottaram || 11 ||

sanskrit_sources.agreement: exact (raw HTML scrape; combined 11-12 page on vedabase; akṣara sequence identical for both verses; this verse's body matches canonical text).
exact (transliteration body identical; punctuation and pāda-break rendering differ between sources).
exact (academic edition; text body identical; punctuation/danda rendering differs).
exact (Sanskrit IAST + word-for-word table identical; bgus presents 6.11-12 jointly; commentaries scope both verses).
exact (academic critical edition; cross-reference Bhg_06.011). Body text identical; word-segmentation and sandhi-rendering choices differ in line with critical-edition conventions.

translations.verbatim_quote: To practice yoga, one should go to a secluded place and should lay kuśa grass on the ground and then cover it with a deerskin and a soft cloth. The seat should be neither too high nor too low and should be situated in a sacred place.

To practice yoga, one should go to a secluded place and should lay kusa grass on the ground and then cover it with a deerskin and a soft cloth. The seat should be neither too high nor too low and should be situated in a sacred place. The yogi should then sit on it very firmly and practice yoga to purify the heart by controlling his mind, senses and activities and fixing the mind on one point.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 1163 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: “Sacred place” refers to places of pilgrimage. In India the yogīs – the transcendentalists or the devotees – all leave home and reside in sacred places such as Prayāga, Mathurā, Vṛndāvana, Hṛṣīkeśa and Hardwar and in solitude practice yoga where the sacred rivers like the Yamunā and the Ganges flow.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 6.11-12 Pratisthapya, having established; sthiram, firmly; sucau, in a clean; dese, place, which is solitary, either naturally or through improvement; atmanah, his own; asanam, seat; na ati ucchritam, neither too high; na ati nicam, nor even too low; and that made of caila-ajina-kusa-uttra…
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-6-11-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Adi Shankaracharya of Advaita Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: 6.11 In a clean place, having established a firm seat — not too high, not too low — covered with kusa grass, a deer-skin, and a cloth, in that order from below.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 6.11 – 6.12 ‘In a clean spot,’ i.e., in a spot pure in itself, not owned or controlled by impure persons and untouched by impure things; having ‘established a firm seat,’ a seat made of wood or similar material, which is neither too high nor too low; which is covered with cloth, deer-skin…
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-6-11-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Ramanuja of Sri Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: 6.11 In a clean place, having set up a steady seat for himself — neither too high nor too low — and having spread on it kuśa grass, a deer-skin and a cloth, in that order from below.

positions.rendering: covered with kusa grass, deer-skin, and cloth, in that order from below (textual reading reversed in compound)
spread on it kuśa grass, deer-skin and cloth, in that order from below
lay kuśa grass on the ground and then cover it with a deerskin and a soft cloth

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: All three traditions read this as literal yogic seat-instruction. The order of layers (kuśa at bottom, deer-skin middle, cloth on top) is what the verse specifies; the compound's textual reading appears reversed but the practical order is universal.

literal_meaning: In a clean place, having established a firm seat for oneself, neither too high nor too low, with cloth-deer-skin-kuśa-grass on top.
traditional_meaning_consensus: Krishna's first piece of somatic instruction: the seat. The conditions are concrete — clean place, firm seat, of moderate height (neither high nor low), prepared with cloth, deer-skin, and kuśa-grass on top. Shankara and Ramanuja both read this literally; this is yogic instruction in the technical sense, anticipating the more elaborated āsana of Patañjali a few centuries later.

remaining_gaps: vedabase.io and bhagavad-gita.us serve 6.11-12 jointly; both pages were captured and the canonical Sanskrit/translation has been split per individual verse using the GRETIL critical edition + canonical-text reference.
